title: Dynamic aggregates in dynamic where clause
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.group_filter/1` that takes params with optional `"min_comments"`, `"min_score"`, `"category_id"`. Build a grouped query (post_id, category_id) with `count(c.id) as comment_count` and `avg(p.score) as avg_score`. For each filter, add a conditional `having` clause via dynamic: `having: ^dynamic_having`. Use `dynamic([p, c], ...)` with aggregate expressions inside. Write to `lib/my_app/blog.ex`.
tier_rationale: Dynamic having with aggregate expressions inside dynamic/2 is one of the rarest Ecto patterns; nearly undocumented.
